task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Share the candies' activity in GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GCompris application open and the 'Share the candies' activity is displayed, featuring a pile of candies and several children.

task_completion.description: Record the initial counts of candies and children in '~/Documents/candy_problem.txt'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or terminal where the agent writes the counts in the format 'Candies: X, Children: Y' to the file '~/Documents/candy_problem.txt'.

task_completion.description: Distribute the candies equally among the children.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively dragging candies from the source pile to the children's areas.
Screenshots show the success state of the activity: the source pile is empty, candies are distributed equally, and a success animation or indicator is visible.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the successful completion to '~/Documents/candy_success.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent using a screenshot tool or command to capture the success screen and saving it specifically as '~/Documents/candy_success.png' in the correct directory.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent clicking and dragging within the GCompris GUI to play the game, rather than bypassing the game logic or faking the success state.

integrity.description: The agent captured a genuine screenshot of the screen.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen using a screenshot utility (like gnome-screenshot, scrot, or a GUI tool) to capture the screen, rather than downloading an image or copying an existing file.

integrity.description: The recorded counts match the actual visual state of the game.
integrity.visual_evidence: The numbers written in 'candy_problem.txt' correspond to the actual number of candies and children visible in the initial state of the GCompris activity.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Share' activity in GCompris teaches division by having users drag and drop candies from a source pile to children's areas to distribute them equally. Success is indicated when the source pile is empty and the children are happy (success animation). The text file must be formatted exactly as 'Candies: X, Children: Y' (case-insensitive). The task uses the real, interactive GCompris application, so the number of candies and children will vary per instance.
